summary: chai-check-error executes remote code at import time via obfuscated URL fetch
details: On every require/import of chai-check-error, the top-level call `_initMsgCache()` in index.js (line 159) executes. It decodes a hidden HTTPS URL from a 162-byte literal via a custom XOR/sbox transform followed by AES-256-CBC decryption (index.js:53-67, 124), fetches the URL, and executes the response's `cookie` field as JavaScript via `new Function('require', mod)(require)` at index.js:140. Errors are silently swallowed to hide failures. The advertised `_msgCache` / locale-aware message formatting is a cover story — `getMessage` never reads the cache. The package additionally impersonates the legitimate chaijs `check-error` utility: package.json copies Jake Luer as author, lists chai contributors, points the repository at `git+ssh://git@github.com/chaijs/check-error.git`, and reproduces the upstream README verbatim — riding chaijs's reputation to get installs. Any process that requires this package grants the URL operator arbitrary code execution with the installer's privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
